race.name: Jackrabbit Marathon
race.tagline: Fifty kilometers through the Laurentian highlands honoring the father of Canadian skiing, Herman 'Jackrabbit' Johannsen.

vitals.aid_stations: Aid stations with warm drinks, snacks, and energy food

climate.description: The Laurentians in early March still have reliable snow cover at elevation. Temperatures range from -15°C to 0°C. March conditions can be variable — from deep winter cold to early spring warmth — but the higher terrain helps preserve snow quality.
challenges: Early March dates mean variable conditions from winter cold to spring warmth
Freeze-thaw cycles possible as spring approaches
Forest canopy creates variable light conditions
Hilly terrain is demanding in soft or icy snow

course.primary: Point-to-point through the Laurentian highlands north of Montreal. The course follows trails blazed in part by Herman 'Jackrabbit' Johannsen, weaving through maple and birch forests with rolling hills typical of the Canadian Shield. Significant cumulative climbing across the 50km distance.
course.surface: Groomed trails through Laurentian highland forest
course.grooming: Groomed by local Nordic clubs with good track quality
features: Named after Herman 'Jackrabbit' Johannsen — the father of Canadian skiing
Laurentian highland terrain with rolling hills and mixed forest
Point-to-point route traversing the heart of Quebec's ski country
Rich French-Canadian ski culture and heritage
Maple and birch forests create beautiful winter scenery
Historic trail connections dating to Johannsen's era

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: A historically significant marathon honoring the legendary Jackrabbit Johannsen. Strong regional prestige in Quebec's skiing community. The Laurentian terrain provides genuine mountain skiing at Canadian Shield altitude.

history.summary: Named after Herman 'Jackrabbit' Johannsen (1875-1987), the Norwegian-born pioneer who is credited with popularizing cross-country skiing in Canada. Johannsen blazed many of the original trails through the Laurentians and skied competitively until well past his 100th birthday. The race celebrates his legacy and the deep French-Canadian ski culture of the Laurentians north of Montreal.
notable_facts: Herman 'Jackrabbit' Johannsen lived to 111 years old and skied past age 100
Johannsen blazed many of the original trails through the Laurentians in the 1920s-30s
The Laurentians are the traditional heartland of Quebec Nordic skiing
The race is conducted primarily in French, reflecting the region's culture
Johannsen was inducted into the Canadian Ski Hall of Fame and is considered the father of Canadian skiing
The Laurentian trails connect historic villages north of Montreal

youtube_data.search_query: Jackrabbit Marathon cross country ski race Laurentians

videos.title: Jack Rabbit   Le Skieur centenaire 1975
videos.description:
Jack Rabbit Johannsen est l'un des grands promoteurs du ski de fond en Amérique du Nord. C'est aussi un homme remarquable dont la vie fut remplie d'aventures de toutes sortes. En voici le portrait fascinant.


This short film retraces the life of Herman Smith Johannsen – the man who introduced the sport of This short film retraces the life of Herman Smith Johannsen – the man who introduced the sport of cross-country skiing to Canadians. From past to present, his life story is portrayed through pictures from sports newsreels, Norwegian archives and his family album. The film catches up with him at both the Canadian Ski Marathon, where he is the honoured guest, and on a return trip to his native Norway.

videos.title: Canadian Ski Marathon
videos.description: The official Canadian Ski Marathon Film 1976
videos.transcript:
at 5 30 on a february morning in a
little town in quebec a spectacular
event is beginning
the largest cross-country ski
competition in north america the
canadian ski
marathon setting off into the darkness
are the first contestants
for the next two days these 600 skiers
carrying heavy packs will try to ski 100
miles in the attempt to earn the coveted
award
the courier dubois
at 8 a.m on the same cold snowy morning
3 000 skiers wait keenly for the
starting signal
every type of skier has entered from the
oldest jack robert johansson at 101 to
the youngest at four years old
so
skiers from as far afield as norway
finland italy and california have come
to test their cross-country skills and
endurance in this two-day event
skiing 50 miles a day they will follow a
well-set trail across rugged canadian
terrain
depending on their skiing ability and
stamina participants can choose from
nine different categories
there are family and school teams
racers and relay teams
and for those six and under there is
even a mighty mites class
the majority of contestants enter as
single tourers for the canadian ski
marathon is an event in which skiers
challenge themselves to ski set
distances within regulation time limits
the marathon trail covers 100 miles of
varied bush land hills woods and rivers
and is divided into 10 10 mile sections
each with its own checkpoints
skiers arrive at checkpoints in
different ways
some rush past with a flourish
others just don't quite make it
huh
canteens at each checkpoint provide
hungry skiers with hot drinks and food
to keep up their stamina
after several hours on the trail it's
nice to have a rest
and make repairs
depending on snow conditions the choice
of ski wax and the method of application
can be critical
everyone has their own technique
with no time to waste the courier dubois
are already preparing to get back on the
course
carrying their regulation 12 pound packs
they must complete 50 miles of trail
today
arriving at checkpoints relay team
skiers find buses waiting to transport
them on to further checkpoints
and tired skiers are taken back to their
hotels for the night
at the end of the first day some skiers
spend the night in the luxury of the
chateau montebello
while the hardy courier dubois
contestants sleep out in the snow
before setting off again at 6am
the other skiers prepare themselves for
the 8am start
weary muscles and sore feet from the
previous day have been revived and
everyone is keen to tackle the second 50
miles
racers are given a three-minute head
start for a clear track
while the other is waiting patiently for
their starting signal
despite the early hour and the nipping
cold most of these skiers look happy
perhaps that is why the marathon motto
is
be cheerful and have a good time
along the trail official skadoos make
safety checks as sudden warm weather can
melt the ice on river crossings
safety is of major importance in the
organization of an event of this size
families make the marathon an enjoyable
weekend outing
for this lad and his grandfather the
beautiful background scenery is an added
bonus
at every checkpoint skiers are checked
in and out by officials who record the
skiers number and time
later
calculations will be made from these
records
all checkpoints have time limits when
the trail closed sign is put up later
rivals are often disappointed to find
that they have been too slow and are not
allowed to continue further on the trail
deep in the woods is checkpoint 10
these courier dubois are relieved to
have arrived at this last checkout point
before the trail is closed at 3 pm
spot checks are made to see that
everyone's pack weighs the regulation 12
pounds
no exceptions allowed
skiers leaving this checkpoint have been
keeping good time for the past 90 miles
the average skier travels three to four
miles an hour taking about three hours
to ski a 10 mile section
but for a racer traveling at 10 to 12
miles an hour a section takes only one
hour
skiers are still keeping up this
exhausting pace as they near the end of
the hundred mile trail
giving a last burst of speed to show the
onlookers that they still have plenty of
energy left the successful skiers arrive
at the finish
some courier dubois winners still look
amazingly fresh and fit
while others are just happy that it's
all over
many skiers will receive distance awards
for completing 60 70 and 80 miles of
trail
and for those who didn't get as far as
their first 50 miles
well
there's always next year's marathon
videos.curation_reason: Official 1976 film showing the Canadian Ski Marathon with detailed course coverage, conditions, and race atmosphere including Jack Rabbit Johannsen.

videos.title: Runners break marathon records
videos.description: Kenyan Japhet Kipkorir and Ethiopian Mulu Seboka have both broken race records in taking out the Melbourne Marathon.
videos.transcript:
it's been a day of milestones for the
Melbourne Marathon more than 27,000
Runners swarmed the city for this
morning's event Kenyon cattle farmer
Jeet koria crossed the line in just over
2 hours and 11 minutes it's the fastest
time ever recorded in the men's event
and amazingly it was only his second
marathon in the women's race the winner
was Ethiopian mulu Soka who smashed the
women's best time by more than a minute

videos.title: Soo Finnish Nordic Ski Club - Jackrabbit Program
videos.description: The Jackrabbit program was recently held at Crimson Ridge Golf Course!
tags: Soo Finnish Nordic Ski Club - Jackrabbit Program
videos.transcript:
the soue Finish Nordic Ski Club recently
held a Jack Robert program at Crimson
Ridge Golf Course the event was a huge
success the uh Jack rabit program is a

the sufis Nordic Ski Club in Sous St
Marie and it's actually a national
program that's run by uh many ski clubs
right across Canada uh it's set out by
the guidelines um set out by uh Cross
Country Canada and cross
Ontario and um it's follows the uh
long-term athlete development program uh
by um set by uh sport Canada and it's uh

jack rabbit program is for 6 to 10 year
olds and then there's a Track Attack
program for 10 to 14 year olds and uh
the Su finish Club also runs uh
additional programs for children Ken and
over who want to pursue skiing more
seriously at that age for Fitness uh or
racing and so we have the racing rabbit
program and ski teams so uh kids can
continue to ski for Fitness or racing uh
right through high school as well the uh
the program gets its name from a man
named Herman Johansson who was a
Norwegian immigrant who is credited for
bringing CrossCountry skiing to Canada
and North America he built the first uh
cross country ski trails near Montreal
in the 1920s and he was given the
nickname Jack Rabbit Johansson so his
legacy lives on in the name of the jack
rabbit program well Crimson Ridge um
invited us here uh for a special field
trip to explore their trails and try out
their terrain Park the uh Jack Rabbit
program uh runs normally out of hawaa
Highlands it runs Saturday mornings uh
from 10: till 12: and we begin uh the
week before Christmas and uh run right
until March break so this is sort of a
little extra field trip for us uh it's
uh very exciting uh everybody seems to
be excited by trying out some new trails
and uh exploring the uh the terrain here
at Crimson uh it's different from hawaa
and I think the combination of uh both
sets of trails is uh is just wonderful
for the skiers well uh skiing like any
sport uh when you start really young and
you develop that physical literacy of
that particular sport then it just
becomes a very natural uh movement and
natural activity uh for the individual
and as the children get older then
they're more efficient uh skiers and it
becomes something that they can do for
life and for Fitness uh I think it's a
great way to uh enjoy the winter get
Outdoors enjoy the fresh air uh it
incorporates a really great um uh
physical fitness uh component in uh in
the winter and it's also a really great
family activity our coaches are uh all
trained through the uh through the Cross
Country Canada and cross country Ontario
coaching training program and so we have
uh several coaches to every group of
children uh skiing there's usually about
eight to 10 children in a group with
anywhere from 2 to four coaches and uh
parent assistants helping if you want
more information on cross country skiing
in Su Mar you can visit W.S finish
nord.com for go Su St Marie I'm David
carasso

quotes.text: At 5:30 on a February morning in a little town in Quebec a spectacular event is beginning - the largest cross-country ski competition in North America, the Canadian Ski Marathon. The marathon trail covers 100 miles of varied bush land, hills, woods and rivers and is divided into 10 sections each with its own checkpoints.
Depending on snow conditions, the choice of ski wax and the method of application can be critical. Everyone has their own technique.
By noon the weather had turned nasty. Despite the weather it was great fun, and Saturday night they would sleep in comfort at an inn in Montebello.
